capabilities.title: Respond to basic questions
capabilities.description: Agent can answer simple questions accurately in interactive mode

capabilities.title: Read and explain own source code
capabilities.description: Agent can read its own files and explain what each part does

capabilities.title: Modify own instructions and prompts
capabilities.description: Agent can improve its own system prompts to be more effective

capabilities.title: Create new custom tools
capabilities.description: Agent can write new tool functions and integrate them into its own toolset

capabilities.title: Error handling and recovery
capabilities.description: Agent handles errors gracefully and can recover from failures

capabilities.title: Execute and validate Python code
capabilities.description: Agent can write Python code, execute it, and validate the output

capabilities.title: Maintain improvement context
capabilities.description: Agent maintains context about past improvements to inform future ones

capabilities.title: Validate own code before writing
capabilities.description: Agent checks code for syntax errors and logical issues before saving

capabilities.title: Refactor and optimize existing code
capabilities.description: Agent can identify and improve inefficient or messy code in its own codebase

capabilities.title: Generate new capabilities autonomously
capabilities.description: Agent can think of new things it should be able to do and add them to the checklist

capabilities.title: Web search and information retrieval
capabilities.description: Add web search capability (e.g. via DuckDuckGo, Tavily, or similar) so Miguel can look up current information, research topics, find documentation, and answer questions about recent events. This is the single biggest gap — without it, Miguel is limited to its training data.

capabilities.title: Persistent memory across sessions
capabilities.description: Implement a memory system that persists across conversations. Should store: key facts learned, user preferences, project context, and past interaction summaries. Could use a local JSON/SQLite store or Agno's built-in memory features. Without this, every conversation starts from zero.

capabilities.title: Structured task planning and decomposition
capabilities.description: Add a planning tool that breaks complex user requests into ordered sub-tasks, tracks progress through them, and handles dependencies. This enables Miguel to tackle multi-step projects (e.g. 'build me an app', 'research and write a report') systematically rather than ad-hoc.

capabilities.title: File analysis — PDF, CSV, images, and structured data
capabilities.description: Add tools to read and analyze common file formats: parse PDFs (text extraction), load and analyze CSVs/Excel files (with pandas), and describe images. This makes Miguel useful for real-world data tasks like 'analyze this spreadsheet' or 'summarize this PDF'.

capabilities.title: API integration framework
capabilities.description: Create a flexible HTTP client tool that can call external REST APIs with configurable auth, headers, and response parsing. Include pre-built integrations for common APIs (weather, news, etc). This lets Miguel interact with the broader world of web services.

capabilities.title: Evolve into an Agno Team with sub-agent delegation
capabilities.description: Refactor Miguel from a single agno.agent.Agent into an agno.team.Team (or equivalent Agno team construct) so Miguel can delegate sub-tasks to specialized sub-agents. Sub-agents get their own fresh context, handle focused work (e.g. 'write this tool file', 'update the README'), and return results to the coordinator. The main Miguel agent becomes the coordinator/orchestrator — it plans, delegates, and assembles. This is the Agno-native way to handle complex tasks without context exhaustion. Modify core.py to create the team structure; sub-agents can share the same model and tools or have focused subsets. Research Agno's team/delegation APIs to find the best approach. This keeps everything within the Agno framework rather than raw API calls.

capabilities.title: Context-aware execution strategy
capabilities.description: Update prompts.py with permanent rules for context management and delegation: (1) When a task is complex, use planning tools to break it into sub-tasks and delegate to sub-agents, (2) Use memory tools to save intermediate results and plans across sub-agent calls, (3) Keep the main agent focused on orchestration — delegate heavy code generation to sub-agents, (4) Write the main implementation code first, then update secondary files (README, architecture, prompts) — never let secondary work crowd out the primary task, (5) Make context-aware delegation a permanent part of every improvement batch. The agent should make this a core part of its self-improvement process so it never exhausts context again.

capabilities.title: Project scaffolding and code generation
capabilities.description: Add the ability to create full project structures — generate directory trees, boilerplate files, config files, and starter code for common project types (Python packages, web apps, scripts, etc). Should write files outside the agent directory when the user requests it. Makes Miguel a practical development assistant. NOTE: Use sub-agent delegation (cap-017) to generate each template in a focused sub-agent to avoid context exhaustion.
